Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to analyze an event log against a normative process description I need to identify which cases deviate from the intended process flow and explain the violations based on the provided text

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Normative Process Description Start Always begins with Receive order Next Step Check inventory must occur before any decisions about payment authorization or fulfillment Sufficient Inventory Path Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order Insufficient Inventory Path Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order Key Constraints Rules Authorize payment only after Check inventory and before Package items Package items only after Authorize payment and before Ship order Ship order only after Package items and never after Cancel order Close order must be the final activity in all scenarios Deviations like shipping after cancellation authorizing payment before checking inventory or packaging before authorization are violations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Sequence Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order Check against rules Matches the Sufficient Inventory Path exactly All constraints met Verdict Compliant

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Sequence Receive order Check inventory Package items Authorize payment Ship order Close order Check against rules Package items occurs before Authorize payment The text explicitly says Package items should only occur after authorization packaging items before authorization would be considered a violation Verdict Violation Packaging before payment authorization

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Sequence Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order Check against rules After Cancel order it proceeds to Ship order The text explicitly says Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Also mixing authorization payment with cancellation is illogical per the description cancellation path should go directly to Close after Cancel Shipping after cancellation is a direct violation Verdict Violation Shipping after cancellation Also authorizing payment before cancellation is contradictory to the described paths but the explicit never after a cancellation rule for shipping is the clearest violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Sequence Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order Check against rules Matches the Insufficient Inventory Path exactly Verdict Compliant

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Sequence Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Ship order Close order Check against rules Skips Package items The text says Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping since items must be prepared before they can be shipped Skipping packaging violates the required sequence Also Ship order should only occur after packaging Verdict Violation Skipping Package items Shipping without packaging

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Address ambiguities if any though the rules are fairly explicit I can note how I interpreted the skip in Case 5 or the mixed path in Case 3

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The prompt mentions potential ambiguities but provides strong explicit constraints I interpreted the constraints strictly because the text uses definitive language must occur should only occur never after would be considered a violation The skipped step in Case 5 is treated as a violation because the text frames packaging as a necessary prerequisite for shipping

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 also has Authorize payment before Cancel order The text says if inventory is insufficient it should directly proceed to Cancel order So authorizing payment before canceling is also a deviation from the described branch logic
